name: Jake's House for Children with Autism - Head Office
Karis Disability Services - Corporate Office
description:
Attending this family camp was truly life-changing for our family. From the moment we arrived, we felt an incredible level of support, understanding, and acceptance. As a large family of children with disabilities, it’s rare to find a space where everyone feels seen, supported, and included and this camp provided exactly that. For the first time in a long time, we felt truly whole. We were able to relax, breathe, and just be a family without the usual stress or worry. The environment allowed us to reconnect with one another, strengthen our bond as a family, and grow closer to God. This experience gave us rest for our minds, hearts, and souls. We are so grateful for the love, care, and community we found here, and we will cherish these memories forever.

name: Achēv

description:
I had a very positive experience with Marilyn Afano as an instructor during my dad’s English assessment. My dad can sometimes be a bit test-anxious and unsure of himself when it comes to language assessments, but Marilyn created such a supportive and comfortable environment that it immediately put him at ease.

What really stood out was how clearly and patiently she explained everything. She didn’t just give instructions quickly and move on—she took the time to go through each part of the assessment step by step, making sure my dad fully understood what was expected. Her explanations were very detailed, which was incredibly helpful for someone who can feel nervous or overwhelmed during testing situations.

She also demonstrated a lot of patience and professionalism. If my dad needed clarification, she was more than willing to explain things again without making him feel rushed or pressured. That level of understanding makes a huge difference for people who may struggle with confidence during language assessments.

Overall, Marilyn Afano is an amazing instructor. She is knowledgeable, patient, and genuinely cares about helping people understand the process. Her ability to communicate clearly and supportively makes the assessment experience much less stressful. I would highly recommend her to anyone who is taking an English assessment and wants someone who truly takes the time to guide and support them.

I would like to thank Assessor Marilyn Alfano for the assessment. The process was clear, fair, and professional. The instructions were easy to understand, and helpful feedback was given.

The assessor was kind, supportive, and knowledgeable. This assessment helped me understand the registration requirements better and supported me in moving forward toward registration.

Thank you for your support and guidance.

Today’s session was very interesting, and I gained valuable insights that I can apply in my life. It will help me build better connections with the Aboriginal community and benefit me both personally and professionally. I would like to attend similar webinars in the future, especially those that focus on understanding the diverse cultures of the Canadian community. Please let me know if any are scheduled. Thank you once again!

I wanted to share my feedback on my recent English test at Achev Agency. The agency is very professional and organized, and Marilyn Alfano, who attended me, was kind, helpful, and made the experience smooth and comfortable. I truly appreciate their support.

name: University Settlement
description:
We come here for swimming and music lessons for our 6 year old daughter. She thoroughly enjoys the classes. Especially the music class.

We like that the location isn’t too far from where we live and that we can walk here. People also just cannot walk in without being buzzed into the building. The change rooms also have a code to prevent unauthorized entry.

I haven’t dealt with the staff personally as my wife deals with them. From what I’ve seen, they are fairly helpful.

Admin staff Renee is very kind and helpful. She answered efficiently about my enquiry. My little daughter Joyce was taken care very well during after school daycare. Highly recommended that.

Pool is closed it seems like 25% of the time. Some months it’s 50%. And if the pool is open often there is no instructor. Communication is spotty at best.

So grateful for all the support our family has received at this incredibly diverse daycare! In the three years my son has been there, we’ve never had any issues. Everyone is very loving, respectful, and caring.

Front desk staff are lovely and fitness room is quiet. However, I won't renew my membership mainly due to hygiene concerns.

I often need to bring empty spray bottles to a staff member to refill, or request paper towels for wiping gym equipment. Haven't had hand sanitizer (fitness room) for months. So, little to no sanitizing before or after equipment use. Unfortunately, most members don't bother - I see visible sweat left on benches/mats that don't get wiped. I've asked for support with messaging but no luck. Meanwhile, I receive countless emails about pool closures and cancelled classes.

I want to like it here and in many ways I do. But they need to drastically lower membership fees for what is offered and ensure basic courtesy and hygiene is practiced. A good start would be supplying members with what is needed to do so.

name: Possibilities Clinic (Ontario Wide Office)
address: 1910 Yonge Street, Suite 115, Toronto, ON M4S 3B2
description:
Before paying out of pocket, people should know that there are free, publicly funded ADHD diagnostic services in Toronto. I wish I had been told this earlier. I spent approximately ~2200/2300 on an assessment at Possibilities Clinic while uninsured. The assessment itself was mostly fine — I had good experiences with Krystal Ratté and Rayna Sansanwal, though my interaction with Shahab Ghorashi was less positive — but the clinic’s administration and follow-up were extremely poor. Phone calls are never answered, email replies are slow and inconsistent, and communication is often handled by a student, which kept me in a loop for months. The most frustrating part was the Disability Tax Credit Certificate (DTC) follow-up. I asked for support with this form from September 10 to November 7, 2024, only to eventually be told the clinic was not equipped to help and that I should refer back to my doctor. This should have been made clear at the outset and would have saved significant time, money, and stress. If you only need testing, this clinic may be adequate. If you need any disability-related documentation or system navigation, get clear written confirmation first — or consider free options instead.

I was a prospective client for ASD assessment. My dog got sick and I was struggling with juggling so many things at a time. I requested the clinic to change my appointment to February 2026 as it’s a big surgery my dog has to go through and I’m not in a position to take on any other thing. These people have no regard for- every other day I am being harassed by them for credit card authorization- I keep telling them I cannot do any assessment until next year but these greedy people don’t get this through their head. Is this compassion? How am I supposed to feel that I am going to get any help from them when all they want is 25 dollars - shame on you for literally harassing me when I’m going through such a hard time. after requesting nicely million times this is what I get. It’s like a robot calling me sounding so happy that my dog is sick. Please coach your harassing calling agents to learn to have compassion. When i said I can’t deal with it for a few weeks i meant it! I’m not running away with 25 dollars - THIS IS NOT MY PRIORITY

A worthwhile service presented with genuine care and empathy.  Every question I had along the way was answered and each interection I had was positive.  I absolutely reccomend their services.

Staff were knowledgeable and kind. They listen more than they speak, are thorough, and always opened up for further questions. Remote appointments allowed for great flexibility as well.

Possibilities Clinic gave me and my family real hope. The whole team was caring and knowledgeable. They didn’t just diagnose ADHD, they guided us every step forward.

name: Ontario Colleges (OCAS)
description:
The non-refundable $110 fee is incredibly predatory in how they enforce it. I only applied for one program (a post-graduate 1 year program, not sure why I even should have to spend an entire application fee on that in the first place) and the program was suspended, so I have no use for the application and want my money back. The person I chatted with online was no help at all and did not care about the issue I was bringing up and just repeatedly would say due to the policy he can't refund me. Considering the ONLY program I applied to was SUSPENDED, I should get my money back because I'm not receiving what I paid for. They didn't have to send transcripts or do anything on their part either so it's just $110 to tell my college I already attend I want to apply for a program, and to get an email back saying it's suspended. $110 down the drain.

They not want to help and if you ask some more questions they not like and rude female Customer service representative. They just want money but not want to provide information. Waste of money rather than apply directly to college. Worse process. Zero stars.

Ontario colleges are scam artists. I have an official transcript that I recieved when I graduated high school in PERFECT condition but they refuse to accept it because it's declared as "unofficial". It's only unofficial because my high school didn't mail it in. What's the difference if I send it in then my high school?? Except for address and envelope there's nothing.  They just wants​ you to pay for them to retrieve it themselves.

The office is listed to be open until 5 pm, but I called at 4:45 pm and got an automated voice message saying that their office is now closed.
Unbelievable!  Are these unionized employees??  If so, no surprise.

A terrible site to have an account!! Because I haven’t used the website in a few years to apply to another college, they erased my whole account, records including my email address with log-in and pass-word as well!

name: Variety Village
description:
I went to watch a friend play VOLT hockey. Variety Village has something for everyone. A really nice facility.

My experience at the Village has been really good. Every time I’m there, it feels welcoming and chill, and everything seems organized and safe. It’s just a nice place to be, and I actually enjoy coming back.

One of the staff I’ve talked to a few times is Aayan. He’s always super friendly and easy to talk to, and he’s really good with the kids. He also knows a lot about the building and the machines/equipment, so if something needs to be set up or someone has a question, he usually has the answer or can help right away. Overall, he’s a solid staff member and you can tell he cares about what he’s doing.

If there’s one word to describe Variety Village, it’s inclusive. This place truly stands out for the way it welcomes and supports everyone. There are so many great activities for adults to enjoy, such as running on the track, swimming, and participating in different fitness programs.
I’ve had the chance to visit Variety Village a few times with Autism Ontario during camp trips, and each experience has been wonderful. One of the things that makes it especially unique is the café, which is open to and supports people with disabilities.
I’d also like to recognize Brian, one of the former supervisors. He’s an incredibly knowledgeable person who genuinely cares about and understands the needs of people with disabilities. His kindness and professionalism made a lasting impression on me.
I truly wish more places were like Variety Village. It’s a perfect example of what inclusion should look like. Unfortunately, not many places offer the same level of accommodation and understanding—but Variety Village sets a great standard for others to follow.

I started out as a member and then went on to volunteer and now I'm am a staff member. Variety Village has been a huge part of my health journey. I walk into the building every day and feel like I'm walking into my home away from home. Variety Village is a community built for everyone. Thank you,  members, volunteers and staff for being part of my amazing and on going health journey ❤️ 🙏 😊 ✨️
